sections.paragraph:
f. (from the root צוּק), distress, Job 15:24 pl. Psa 25:17.

I. מָצֹור m. with suff. מְצוּרֶךָ Eze 4:8 (from the root צוּר).-(a) distress, Deu 28:53, seq.-(b) siege, Eze 4:2, Eze 4:7. בֹּוא בַמָּצֹור to be besieged (of a city), 2Ki 24:10 25:2 .-(c) a mound, raised by besiegers, Deu 20:20 Mic. 4:14 .-(d) bulwark, citadel, 2Ch 32:10 Hab 2:1. More often מָצֹור עִיר a fortified city, Psa 31:22 60:11 2Ch 8:5.

II. מָצֹור pr.n. of Egypt, apparently of lower Egypt, יְאֹרֵי מָצור the rivers or channels of Egypt (the branches of the Nile), Isa 19:6 37:25 2Ki 19:24. Whatever be said as to its Egyptian origin, the Hebrews probably took this word in the signification of borders or limit, i.q. مِصْرُ, as if it were the sing. of the noun מִצְרַיִם twofold Egypt, which see. Others, as Bochart, in Phaleg. iv. 24, regard Egypt as so called from its being strong and fortified (see Died. i. 31). [This name is supposed to be a Hebraized form of the Egyptian ⲙⲉⲧⲟϫⲣⲟ, kingdom. Thes.].
